title: Regolith-Ablation Beam Splitter: Phase-Mask Design for a Mining Spot Array
strategies: superposition-of-gratings DOE: sum a blazed grating per target spot and take the phase (the reference; interference makes spots non-uniform)
random-phase superposition or a few Gerchberg-Saxton back-and-forth steps to break destructive interference
iterative Fourier-transform (Gerchberg-Saxton): enforce far-field amplitude on the target spots, keep aperture phase, iterate to concentrate power
multi-restart weighted / adaptive-additive GS that boosts the dimmest spots and keeps the best mask found, trading efficiency against uniformity
